zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth van Brabant於1214年出生在比利時布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen54歲，母親Marie de France17歲。她於1233年3月19日與Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken在比利時布拉班特洛文結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1272年10月23日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福維謝爾享年58歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福維謝爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth van Brabant于1214年出生在比利时布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen54岁，母亲Marie de France17岁。她于1233年3月19日与Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken在比利时布拉班特洛文结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1272年10月23日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福維謝爾享年58岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福維謝爾。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth van Brabant was born in 1214, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium, her father, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, was 54 and her mother, Marie de France, was 17. She married Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken on 19 March 1233, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 23 October 1272, in Wissel, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 58, and was buried in Wissel, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth van Brabant est née en 1214, à Louvain, Brabant, Belgique, son père, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Marie de France, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken le 19 mars 1233, à Leuven, Brabant, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 octobre 1272, à Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth van Brabant 1214 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen 54 und ihre Mutter Marie de France 17. Sie hat am 19. März 1233 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 23. Oktober 1272 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth van Brabant è nata il nel 1214 nel Leuven, Brabante, Belgio, sua padre, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Marie de France, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken il 19 marzo 1233, nel Leuven, Brabante, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 23 ottobre 1272, nel Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolta a Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth van Brabantが1214年にベルギーブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenは54歳，母Marie de Franceは17歳であった。1233年3月19日にベルギーブラバントレウベンでThierry of cleves lord of dinslakenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1272年10月23日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブウィセルで58歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブウィセルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth van Brabant(이)가 1214년 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie de France(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1233년 3월 19일 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1272년 10월 23일 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브 와이셀에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브 와이셀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth van Brabant nasceu em 1214, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, seu pai, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Marie de France, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken em 19 de março de 1233, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1272, em Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth van Brabant родилась в 1214 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Marie de France, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken 19 марта 1233 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 23 октября 1272 г. в местности виссел, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности виссел, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth van Brabant nació en 1214, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, su papá, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Marie de France, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Thierry of cleves lord of dinslaken el 19 de marzo de 1233, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1272, en Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en Wissel, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
